Apple___Apple_scab.symptoms: Olive-green to brown velvety spots on leaves and fruit. Leaves may curl and drop prematurely.
Apple___Apple_scab.organic_treatment: Apply neem oil spray, sulfur-based fungicides, or baking soda solution (1 tbsp/gallon water).
Apple___Apple_scab.chemical_treatment: Captan 50% WP @ 2g/L or Mancozeb 75% WP @ 2.5g/L spray at 10-15 day intervals.
Apple___Apple_scab.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Rake and destroy fallen leaves. Ensure good air circulation by pruning.

Apple___Black_rot.symptoms: Brown, expanding lesions with concentric rings ('frog-eye' pattern) on leaves. Fruit develops firm, black rot.
Apple___Black_rot.organic_treatment: Prune infected branches. Apply copper-based fungicide. Remove mummified fruit.
Apple___Black_rot.chemical_treatment: Captan 50% WP or Thiophanate-methyl @ 1g/L. Apply from bud break.
Apple___Black_rot.prevention: Remove all dead wood and mummified fruit. Maintain proper tree sanitation. Avoid wounding bark.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surface. Tube-like projections on leaf undersides.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.organic_treatment: Remove nearby cedar/juniper trees (alternate host). Apply sulfur sprays.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.chemical_treatment: Myclobutanil or Triadimefon @ 1ml/L spray before infection period.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.prevention: Plant rust-resistant apple varieties. Remove junipers within 2-mile radius if possible.

Apple___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms. Healthy green leaves with normal growth.
Apple___healthy.organic_treatment: No treatment needed. Continue regular maintenance.
Apple___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper nutrition, watering, and pruning schedule.

Blueberry___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms. Healthy plant with normal foliage.
Blueberry___healthy.prevention: Maintain acidic soil pH (4.5-5.5), adequate mulching and irrigation.

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves, shoots and fruit. Leaves curl upward. Stunted growth.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.organic_treatment: Neem oil spray, potassium bicarbonate, or milk solution (1:9 ratio with water).
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.chemical_treatment: Sulfur 80% WP @ 3g/L or Trifloxystrobin @ 0.5ml/L at first sign of infection.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Ensure good air circulation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Plant resistant varieties.

Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms. Healthy cherry plant.
Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.prevention: Regular pruning, adequate watering, balanced fertilization.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.symptoms: Rectangular gray to tan lesions between veins. Lesions may coalesce causing leaf blight.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.organic_treatment: Rotate crops. Use compost tea sprays. Improve air circulation in canopy.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.chemical_treatment: Mancozeb 75% WP @ 2.5g/L or Propiconazole @ 1ml/L at first appearance.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.prevention: Crop rotation (2-3 year). Tillage to bury residue. Plant resistant hybrids.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.symptoms: Small, circular to elongated cinnamon-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.organic_treatment: Remove severely infected leaves. Apply sulfur-based fungicides.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids. Early planting can often avoid peak rust periods.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.symptoms: Long (1-6 inch), cigar-shaped gray-green lesions on leaves. Severe cases defoliate the plant.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.organic_treatment: Crop rotation, deep plowing of crop residue after harvest.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.chemical_treatment: Propiconazole 25% EC @ 1ml/L or Azoxystrobin @ 1ml/L at disease onset.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids. Rotate crops. Practice balanced fertilization.

Corn_(maize)___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms. Healthy maize plant.
Corn_(maize)___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper spacing, nutrition, and weed management.

Grape___Black_rot.symptoms: Brown circular spots on leaves. Berries shrivel into hard, black mummies.
Grape___Black_rot.organic_treatment: Remove mummified berries. Apply copper-based fungicide in early season.
Grape___Black_rot.chemical_treatment: Mancozeb 75% WP @ 2.5g/L or Myclobutanil @ 1ml/L starting before bloom.
Grape___Black_rot.prevention: Remove all mummified fruit. Prune for airflow. Destroy infected debris.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).symptoms: Tiger-striped leaves with interveinal chlorosis/necrosis. Dark spots on berries. Trunk cankers.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).organic_treatment: Prune and remove infected wood. Apply wound sealant. Use Trichoderma biocontrol.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).chemical_treatment: No fully effective chemical treatment. Sodium arsenite was used historically (now banned).
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).prevention: Prune during dry weather. Avoid large wound sites. Protect wounds with sealant.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.symptoms: Irregular brown to dark purple spots on leaves. Severe cases cause premature defoliation.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.organic_treatment: Apply neem oil or copper oxychloride spray.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.prevention: Improve air circulation. Remove fallen leaves. Avoid overhead irrigation.

Grape___healthy.prevention: Regular pruning, balanced fertilization, proper trellis management.

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).symptoms: Asymmetric blotchy mottling of leaves. Lopsided, bitter fruit. Yellow shoots. Tree decline.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).organic_treatment: Remove infected trees. Control Asian citrus psyllid with neem oil or insecticidal soap.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).chemical_treatment: No cure exists. Control vector psyllid with Imidacloprid or Thiamethoxam.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).prevention: Use certified disease-free nursery stock. Monitor and control psyllid populations aggressively.

Peach___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves. Fruit develops sunken, cracked lesions.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.organic_treatment: Copper hydroxide spray at leaf fall and pre-bloom. Prune for air circulation.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.chemical_treatment: Oxytetracycline or copper-based bactericide @ 2g/L during growing season.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid overhead irrigation. Maintain tree vigor.

Peach___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms. Healthy peach tree.
Peach___healthy.prevention: Regular pruning, thinning fruit, balanced fertilization.

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves that become brown lesions. Fruit has raised scabby spots.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.organic_treatment: Copper-based fungicide spray. Remove infected plant debris. Use compost tea.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use disease-free seeds. Crop rotation (3 years). Avoid working in wet fields.

Pepper,_bell___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms. Healthy pepper plant.
Pepper,_bell___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper spacing, nutrition, and pest management.

Potato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark brown concentric rings ('target spots') on older leaves. Lower leaves affected first.
Potato___Early_blight.organic_treatment: Apply neem oil spray. Use compost to boost plant immunity. Remove infected leaves.
Potato___Early_blight.chemical_treatment: Mancozeb 75% WP @ 2.5g/L or Chlorothalonil @ 2g/L at 7-10 day intervals.
Potato___Early_blight.prevention: Crop rotation (3 years). Use certified seed. Adequate spacing. Avoid overhead irrigation.

Potato___Late_blight.symptoms: Water-soaked gray-green spots rapidly expanding. White mold on leaf undersides in humid conditions.
Potato___Late_blight.organic_treatment: Copper-based fungicide. Remove and destroy infected plants immediately.
Potato___Late_blight.chemical_treatment: Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (Ridomil Gold) @ 2.5g/L or Cymoxanil + Mancozeb @ 3g/L.
Potato___Late_blight.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid overhead irrigation. Destroy all cull piles and volunteers.

Potato___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms. Healthy potato plant.
Potato___healthy.prevention: Use certified seed potatoes. Maintain proper hilling and irrigation.

Raspberry___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms. Healthy raspberry plant.
Raspberry___healthy.prevention: Regular pruning, proper trellising, and adequate spacing.

Soybean___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms. Healthy soybean plant.
Soybean___healthy.prevention: Crop rotation, use treated seed, maintain field drainage.

Squash___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery fungal growth on upper leaf surfaces. Leaves yellow and die prematurely.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.organic_treatment: Neem oil, potassium bicarbonate, or milk spray (1:9). Sulfur dust application.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.chemical_treatment: Myclobutanil @ 1ml/L or Chlorothalonil @ 2g/L at 10-14 day intervals.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Ensure good air circulation. Avoid excessive nitrogen.

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.symptoms: Irregular purple spots on leaves, expanding to cover leaf. Leaf margins appear scorched.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.organic_treatment: Remove infected leaves. Apply copper-based fungicide. Improve air circulation.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.chemical_treatment: Captan 50% WP @ 2g/L or Myclobutanil @ 1ml/L starting at first sign.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.prevention: Remove old infected leaves after harvest. Use resistant varieties. Proper spacing.

Strawberry___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms. Healthy strawberry plant.
Strawberry___healthy.prevention: Mulching, proper spacing, drip irrigation, renovation after harvest.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small dark raised spots on leaves and fruit. Leaves become yellow and drop.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.organic_treatment: Copper hydroxide spray. Remove infected plants. Use compost tea as foliar spray.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use disease-free seeds/transplants. Crop rotation (2-3 years). Avoid overhead watering.

Tomato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark concentric rings (target board pattern) on lower leaves first. Stem lesions may girdle plant.
Tomato___Early_blight.organic_treatment: Neem oil spray. Remove lower infected leaves. Mulch to prevent soil splash.
Tomato___Early_blight.chemical_treatment: Mancozeb 75% WP @ 2.5g/L or Chlorothalonil 75% WP @ 2g/L. Alternate fungicides.
Tomato___Early_blight.prevention: Stake/cage plants for airflow. Mulch soil surface. 3-year crop rotation. Use resistant varieties.

Tomato___Late_blight.symptoms: Dark brown spots on leaves with white fungal growth on undersides. Rapid plant collapse in wet weather.
Tomato___Late_blight.organic_treatment: Apply copper-based fungicides. Remove and destroy all infected plants immediately.
Tomato___Late_blight.chemical_treatment: Mancozeb 75% WP @ 2g/L or Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (Ridomil Gold) @ 2.5g/L.
Tomato___Late_blight.prevention: Use disease-resistant varieties. Ensure proper spacing. Avoid overhead irrigation. Remove volunteers.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.symptoms: Pale yellow spots on upper leaf surface. Olive-green velvety mold on leaf undersides.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.organic_treatment: Improve ventilation in greenhouse. Apply neem oil or potassium bicarbonate.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.prevention: Reduce humidity below 85%. Ensure good air circulation. Avoid overhead watering.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.symptoms: Numerous small circular spots with dark margins and gray centers on leaves. Lower leaves first.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.organic_treatment: Remove infected lower leaves. Apply copper fungicide. Mulch around plants.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.chemical_treatment: Mancozeb 75% WP @ 2.5g/L or Chlorothalonil at 7-10 day intervals.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.prevention: Avoid overhead irrigation. Stake plants. Crop rotation (3 years). Remove crop debris.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.symptoms: Tiny yellow spots (stippling) on leaves. Fine webbing on leaf undersides. Leaf bronzing and drying.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.organic_treatment: Spray strong water jets to dislodge mites. Apply neem oil or insecticidal soap.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.chemical_treatment: Abamectin @ 0.5ml/L or Spiromesifen @ 0.8ml/L. Rotate acaricides to prevent resistance.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.prevention: Maintain adequate humidity. Avoid excessive nitrogen. Encourage predatory mites (Phytoseiulus).

Tomato___Target_Spot.symptoms: Small brown circular spots with concentric rings, resembling targets. Can affect leaves, stems, fruit.
Tomato___Target_Spot.organic_treatment: Remove infected leaves. Apply copper-based fungicide. Improve air circulation.
Tomato___Target_Spot.chemical_treatment: Chlorothalonil 75% WP @ 2g/L or Azoxystrobin @ 1ml/L at first symptoms.
Tomato___Target_Spot.prevention: Proper plant spacing. Avoid overhead irrigation. Remove crop debris after season.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.symptoms: Severe leaf curling and yellowing. Stunted plant growth. Reduced fruit production.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.organic_treatment: Control whitefly vector with yellow sticky traps and neem oil spray. Remove infected plants.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.chemical_treatment: Control whitefly with Imidacloprid @ 0.3ml/L or Thiamethoxam @ 0.3g/L.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.prevention: Use virus-resistant varieties. Install insect-proof nets. Control whitefly populations early.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.symptoms: Mosaic pattern of light/dark green on leaves. Leaf distortion. Reduced fruit quality and yield.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.organic_treatment: Remove and destroy infected plants. Disinfect tools with 10% bleach solution.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.chemical_treatment: No direct chemical treatment for virus. Control aphid vectors with Imidacloprid.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention: Use virus-free seed. Wash hands before handling plants. Disinfect tools. Control aphids.

Tomato___healthy.symptoms: No disease symptoms. Healthy tomato plant with normal green foliage.
Tomato___healthy.organic_treatment: No treatment needed. Continue regular maintenance and nutrition.
Tomato___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper watering, fertilization, and pest monitoring schedule.
